titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Enrico Scuro Photographs
titlestmt.author: by Sandrine Guérin

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 16875628.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid written in English and Italian.

Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 26, 2024 at 03:18

did.unittitle: Enrico Scuro Photographs

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Titles and captions on photographs in Italian.

did.abstract: The collection consists of 456 black-and-white and color inkjet prints by Italian photographer Enrico Scuro that document Movimento '77 and other social, cultural, and political events in Italy, 1976-1981.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from S. T. di Matteo di Castro on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized by the photographer chronologically and thematically around themes. Prints numbered by the photographer and arranged in sequential order.

bioghist.head: Enrico Scuro (1952-)
bioghist.p: Enrico Scuro (born 1952, Taranto, Italy). Italian photographer who documented social and political events in Italy in the 1970s including Movimento del '77. Scuro moved to Bologna at the age of twenty and enrolled in Discipline delle arti, della musica e dello spettacolo (DAMS) at the University of Bologna, where he studied photography under Italo Zannier. He chronicled the period of social and political unrest that swept through Bologna and the rest of Italy in the 1970s. He later collaborated with the photo agency Grazia Neri as a freelance photographer.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Enrico Scuro Photographs. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

The guide to the collection includes Italian titles derived from inscriptions on versos of prints as well as from the photographer's website https://enricoscuro.it, accessed in February 2023, in addition to English translations for most.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated Materials: Living Theater Records (YCAL MSS 1038). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of 456 black-and-white and color inkjet prints of photographs by Italian photographer Enrico Scuro that document Movimento '77 and other social, cultural, and political events in Italy, 1976-1981.
The following is a summary of the main series and their contents. Photographs document:
- Radio Alice and creative Autonomia: photographs of political happenings and of a trip to Germany by member of the free radio after it was shut down by the police.
- Countercultural youth gatherings: Proletarian Youth Festival at Parco Lambro, Milan, and Umbria Jazz Youth Festival in 1976, and a festival in the Abruzzo National Park, 1977.

- Movimento '77 and related protests: occupation of university buildings, demonstrations, construction of parade props. Riots and mass protest following the death of Francesco Lorusso. Political happenings, parades, gatherings, and a march following the death of Giorgiana Masi in May 1977. Photographs of the International Congress against Repression in Bologna in September 1977.
- Marches and demonstrations following the kidnapping and killing of Aldo Moro in May 1978.

- Exhibitions and performances, including concerts by Skiantos and Patti Smith, performances by John Cage, Julian Beck and Judith Malina from the Living Theater, an exhibition by Fluxus artists and other cultural events associated with the avant-garde, 1978-1979.
- Organized squats of unoccupied buildings by the Tenants Union, 1981.

Notable subjects in the collection include Nanni Moretti, Dario Luigi, Angelo Fo, Tomás Maldonado, Umberto Eco, Luigi Squarzina, Julian Beck and Judith Malina from the Living Theater, Fluxus artists, and John Cage.

Inkjet prints on baryta paper, 7 ¾ x 11 ¾ inches (20 X 30 centimeters). Chiefly in black-and-white with a few prints in color (numbered 398-402, 422-424). Titled, numbered, and stamped on the verso by Enrico Scuro. Printed approximately 2021.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Enrico Scuro Photographs is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: Università di Bologna. Dipartimento di musica e spettacolo

did.unittitle: Radio Alice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs depicting political happenings organized by Radio Alice, a free radio broadcasting from Bologna, Italy, from February 1976 to 1979, and the related autonomist magazine A/traverso .

did.unittitle: Party for Radio Alice at Margherita Gardens, Bologna / Festa per Radio Alice ai Giardini Margherita
Let's celebrate repression / Facciamo festa alle repressioni
Parco Lambro Youth Festival

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of youth participating in communal activities, playing music, and attending concerts, in the nude.

did.unittitle: Proletarian Youth Festival at Lambro Park, Milan / Festival del proletariato giovanile al Parco Lambro, Milano

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Festival del Proletariato Giovanile was an annual music festival organized by counterculture magazine Re Nudo , 1971-1976, and held at Parco Lambro in Milan from 1974 to 1976.

did.unittitle: Umbria Jazz Youth Festival

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting Umbria Jazz, a free music festival at towns in the Umbrianregion of Italy, July 20-25, 1976

did.unittitle: Orvieto; Perugia
Gubbio
Citta di Castello
Castiglione del Lago
Villalago-Terni
Perugia
Movimento del '77 - Demonstration Against the Criminalization of the Movement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting a demonstration denouncing the criminalization of autonomist movements.

did.unittitle: Demonstration against the criminalization of student struggles / "No alla criminalizzazione delle lotte"
Movimento del '77 - Occupation of Universities

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting the occupation by students and militants of the Discipline delle arti, della musica e dello spettacolo (DAMS) in Bologna and at other universities.

did.unittitle: Dragon preparation at DAMS / Preparazione drago al DAMS

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Construction of parade props at the occupied Discipline di Arti, Musica e Spettacolo (DAMS) in Bologna, including the Chinese dragon that will become a symbol of Movimento '77. Prints numbered 78-81

did.unittitle: Graffiti in the occupied DAMS / Scritte al DAMS occupato

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Graffiti in the occupied Discipline di Arti, Musica e Spettacolo (DAMS) in Bologna. Prints numbered 82-118

did.unittitle: Assembly at the Cinema Odeon / Assemblea al Cinema Odeon
Graffiti in DAMS / Scritte al DAMS

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Graffiti in the occupied Discipline di Arti, Musica e Spettacolo (DAMS) in Bologna. Prints numbered 120-121

did.unittitle: Celebration at DAMS / Festa al DAMS

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Parties at the occupied Discipline di Arti, Musica e Spettacolo (DAMS) in Bologna. Prints numbered 122-127

did.unittitle: The dragon of DAMS / Il drago del DAMS

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: To circumvent the ban on demonstrations, a procession is held on Carnival Sunday featuring the Chinese dragon built in the occupied Discipline di Arti, Musica e Spettacolo (DAMS). The Chinese dragon will become the symbol of the Bologna movement. The Chinese dragon, also known as the loong, long or lung, is a legendary creature in Chinese mythology, Chinese folklore, and Chinese culture at large.

did.unittitle: Metropolitan Indians / Indiani metropolitani

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Indiani Metropolitani were members of creative autonomia. Their actions promoted "stare insieme" (be together), linguistic experimentation, and the arts, especially music.

did.unittitle: Assembly in the department of Literature / Assemblea alla Facoltà di Lettere
Assembly at DAMS / Assemblea al DAMS

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Umberto Eco and Luigi Squarzina at the occupied Discipline delle arti, della musica e dello spettacolo (DAMS). Prints numbered 150, 154, 155, and Tomás Maldonado, print numbered 153. Prints numbered 148-155

did.unittitle: Movimento del '77 - Mass protests and riots following the death of Francesco Lorusso

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting demonstrations and riots in Bologna following the killing of Lotta Continua militant Francesco Lorusso by carabiniere Massimo Tramontani on March 11, 1977.

did.unittitle: Bullet holes / Fori di proiettile in Via Mascarella, Bologna

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph of bullet holes in wall where Francesco Lorusso was killed, via Mascarella. Print numbered 156

did.unittitle: National protest, Rome / Manifestazione Nazionale del Movimento degli Studenti, Roma

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of national protest in Rome following the killing of Francesco Lorusso by carabiniere Massimo Tramontani. Prints numbered 157-159

did.unittitle: Clashes in via Zamboni / Scontri in via Zamboni, Bologna
The Funeral of Francesco Lorusso / I Funerali di Francesco Lorusso
Sit-in via Rizzoli, Bologna
Student protest during union rally / "Vogliamo parlare", Manifestazione sindacale in Piazza Maggiore, Bologna
Demonstration in Piazza Verdi / Controinformazione in Piazza Verdi, Bologna

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Demonstration in Piazza Verdi to denounce indiscriminate arrests by police. Prints numbered 170-175

did.unittitle: Murals / Murale Academia, Bologna
Murals / Murale via Zamboni, Bologna
Political happening in Piazza Maggiore / Controinformazione in Piazza Maggiore, Bologna

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting street happenings and processions organized by the student movement. Prints numbered 188-194

did.unittitle: Movimento del '77 - Political happenings, parades, and gatherings

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Activists circumvented the ban on demonstrations imposed by the government by organizing carnivalesque parades and political happenings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting political happenings, processions with musical bands, and a march honoring student Giorgiana Masi following her death in Rome on May 13, 1977.

did.unittitle: Festa al palazzo Re Enzo, "Riprendiamoci la vita", Bologna

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Activists circumvented the ban on demonstrations imposed by the government by organizing carnivalesque parades and political happenings

did.unittitle: Festa, "In giro con la banda", Bologna
Corteo Andreotti e Berlinguer "Oggi Sposi", Bologna
Associazione nazionale studenti, Bologna
Corteo per Giorgiana Masi, Bologna
Abbiamo murato il covo del rettore, Bologna
Giornata di festa e di lotta, Bologna
Dario Fo and Franca Rame al Pasaporte
Movimento del '77 - Radio Alice in Germany

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting a trip to Germany by members of Radio Alice, a free radio from Bologna.

did.unittitle: Radio Alice in Nuremberg
Radio Alice in Munich
Radio Alice in Berlin
Radio Alice in East Berlin and East Germany
Radio Alice in Hamburg
Radio Alice in Göttingen
Radio Alice in Bremen
Radio Alice in Frankfurt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photograph of Maurizio Torrealta, print numbered 251. Prints numbered 251-255

did.unittitle: Movimento del '77 - Protesting the Incarceration of Comrades

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs depicting activists who chained themselves to protest the investigation against Radio Alice and incarceration of Bruno Giorgini, Diego Benecchi, Francesco "Bifo" Berardi, Angelo Pasquini and others.

did.unittitle: Chained up / Incatenati
Youth Festival in Abruzzo Park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting the youth festival "L'Orso con il Sacco a Pelo con la Chitarra" ("Bear with a Sleeping Bag and Guitar") at the Parco Nazionale d'Abruzzo, Lazio e Molise.

did.unittitle: Youth Festival / L'Orso, Villavallelonga (L'Aquila, Abruzzo)
Movimento del '77 - The "Convention against Repression," Bologna

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting "Il Convegno contro la repressione" ("Convention against Repression"), a conference held in Bologna, September 22-24, 1977, during which youth as well as members and sympathizers of various left-wing groups from all over Italy gathered and protested against state repression.

did.unittitle: Convention against repression / Il Convegno contro la repressione, assemblea al Palasport
Convention against repression / Il Convegno contro la repressione
Convention / Convegno, Dario Fo
Movimento del '77 - Graffiti and manifesto

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting graffiti, murals, and posters in the streets related to Movimento '77.

did.unittitle: Graffiti / Scritte, Bologna
Manifesto su Catalanotti, Bologna
Graffiti / Scritte, Bologna
Graffiti feminist / Scritte Femministe, Bologna
Manifesto, Bologna
Graffiti / Scritte, Bologna
Francesco Lorusso Tombstone / Lapide [Francesco] Lorusso, Bologna
Manifesto Lorusso, Bologna
Graffiti manifesto / Scritte Manifesto, Bologna
Manifesto Lorusso, Bologna
Graffiti / Scritte, Bologna
"Raising funds" / "Raccontato Fondi", Bologna

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sign that reads "Raccontato Fondi" ("raising funds"). Print numbered 329

did.unittitle: Movimento del '77 - Sit-in

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting a sit-in protest in front of the tribunal in Bologna.

did.unittitle: Sit-in in front of tribunal to request trial / Sit-in davanti al Tribunale per richiedere il processo
Movimento del '77 - Play at Palasport

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting a theatrical play, La vita, catalanotti e noi: gli atti di una inquisizione ("Life, Catalanotti and us: the proceedings of an inquisition"), performed at Palasport with the participation of Italian politician Domenico Mimmo Pinto.

did.unittitle: A play at Palasport "The proceedings of an inquisition" / Sceneggiata al Palasport, "gli atti di una inquisizione"
Skiantos in concert under San Donato bridge

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting a party and concert by punk band Skiantos under the San Donato Bridge in Bologna, Itlay.

did.unittitle: Party at San Donato, Skiantos / Festa a San Donato, Skiantos
Movimento del '77 - Nanni Moretti at DAMS

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portraits of film director and actor Giovanni "Nanni" Moretti at the occupied Discipline delle arti, della musica e dello spettacolo (DAMS), in Bologna.

did.unittitle: Nanni Moretti at DAMS / Nanni Moretti al Dams
Movimento del '77 - Trial for the March '77 events
Trial for the March '77 events / Il processo per i fatti di marzo, Bologna
Reaction to the assassination of Aldo Moro

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Aldo Moro (1916-1978) was a former Italian Prime Minister and prominent member of the Christian Democrat (DC) party. Moro was kidnapped in Rome on March 16, 1978, by the Red Brigades, a far-left Marxist-Leninist organiztion. After negotiations with the government failed, the terrorists killed Moro. His body was found on May 9, 1978, in the trunk of a car near the Italian Communist Party (PCI) headquarters in Rome.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting events in Rome following the assassination of Italian politician Aldo Moro by the Red Brigades.

did.unittitle: "They killed Aldo Moro" / "Hanno ucciso Aldo Moro", Sezione PCI

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sign that reads: "Hanno ucciso Aldo Moro. Alle ore 16.00 manifestiamo la nostra ferma volonta di difendere la democrazia, tutti a San Giovanni contro il terrorismo e la barbarie" in front of the headquarters of the Sezione PCI - Regola Campitelli. Print numbered 345

did.unittitle: Aldo Moro, Via Gaetani
Aldo Moro, Piazza del Gesù
Aldo Moro, Manifestazione sindacale
Aldo Moro, Luciano Lama parla a Piazza San Giovanni
Aldo Moro, Manifestazione sindacale

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: "Piazza San Giovanni, Manifestazione sindacale CGIL CISL UIL contro il terrorismo e la violenza eversiva, per la difesa della democrazia e delle istituzioni"

did.unittitle: Aldo Moro, Corteo della Democrazia Cristiana
Movimento del '77 - Release from prison

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting the release of Movimento '77 activists

did.unittitle: Release from prison / Uscita dal carcere
Fluxus and the International Week of Performance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting the exhibition Fluxus come Fluxus ( Fluxus as Fluxus ) and the International Week of Performance at the Gallery of Modern Art, Bologna.

did.unittitle: Exhibition Fluxus as Fluxus / Mostra Fluxus come Fluxus

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: METAFISICA DEL QUOTIDIANO ( Metaphysics of the Everyday ) curated by Franco Solmi. Section Fluxus come Fluxus ( Fluxus as Fluxus ) curated by Achille Bonito Oliva, Gallery of Modern Art, Bologna. June 1-August 31, 1978. Works and performance by George Brecht, George Maciunas, Bob Watts, Daniel Spoerri, Ben Vautier, Robert Filliou, Wolf Vostell, Nam June Paik, Yoko Ono, Dick Higgins, Astrit Schmidt-Burkhardt, Joseph Beuys, Arthur Köpke, Whitman, Takehisa Kosugi, Milan Knizak, Giuseppe Chiari, Walter Marchetti, Gianni-Emilio Simonetti

did.unittitle: International Week of Performance / II Settimana Internazionale della Performance

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Settimana Internazionale della Performance alla Galleria d'Arte Moderna, Bologna

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Identified persons include: Giorgio Barberio Corsetti (print 390), Gianni Colosimo (prints 394-395), Jean-Paul Curtay (print 387), Francesco Dal Bosco (prints 388-389), François Dufrêne (print 391), Michel Laub (prints 396-397) Maurice Lemaître (prints 386, 402), Massimo Mostacchi (prints 393, 401), Cristina Roncati (prints 383-384), Fried Rosenstock (prints 382, 398), Adriano Spatola (prints 385, 399), Cesare Viviani (print 392). Group photo of Francesco Dal Bosco, Giorgio Barberio Corsetti, and Fabrizio Varesco (print 400). Print 398-402 are in color

did.unittitle: Movimento del '77 - Clashes During the National Democracy Rally

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting clashes at the National Democracy Rally (Democrazie Nationale, DN).

did.unittitle: Elections 1978, Clashes during the National Democracy Rally / Elezioni 1978, scontri al comizio di Democrazia Nazionale
John Cage happening in Bologna

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting the first performance of Il Treno de John Cage on the Bologna-Porretta line on June 26, 1978.

did.unittitle: John Cage's Train / Il Treno de John Cage

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Cage's Train. In Search of Lost Silence was an artistic-musical performance that combined man-made sounds and environmental sounds, produced by the train and by the people present. The performance was commissioned by Tito Gotti. John Cage directed the performance with the assistance of Juan Hidalgo and Walter Marchetti. The performance took place during three train excursions: the Bologna-Porretta line on June 26; the Bologna-Ravenna line on June 27, and the Ravenna-Rimini line on June 28.

did.unittitle: Santarcangelo International Theatre Festival

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph of festival attendees practicing meditation in the nude.

did.unittitle: Meditation / Meditazione, fiume nei pressi di Sant'Arcangelo di Romagna

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph of festival attendees practicing meditation in the nude. Print numbered 411

did.unittitle: Movimento del '77 - Clashes between the Italian Communist Party and Youth and Militants

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting clashes between the Italian Communist Party (Partito Comunista Italiano, PCI) and Movimento del '77 youth and militants in Bologna.

did.unittitle: Clashes / Scontri in piazza Verdi e via Zamboni
Living Theatre in Bologna

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Living Theatre was founded in 1947 by Julian Beck and Judith Malina in New York City. The company collaborated with other avant-garde and radical cultural and political movements in the United States and Europe from the 1960s to the present. The company performed political plays commenting on war, Wall Street, and income inequality, and members have been arrested during public performances. After Julian Beck's death in 1985, Hanon Reznikov was named codirector. Reznikov died in 2008 and Malina in 2015. The Living Theatre continues with artistic director Brad Burgess to conduct workshops and perform in the United States and abroad.

did.unittitle: Julian Beck and Judith Malina, Living Theater
Skiantos Concert

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of Italian punk band Skiantos on stage, with band members getting undressed, hanging clothes on a cloth line, using a broomstick as guitar.

did.unittitle: Skiantos in Bologna

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black-and-white prints numbered 419-421. Color prints numbered 422-424

did.unittitle: Patti Smith Concert

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of youth Piazza Maggiore waiting for a concert by Patti Smith.

did.unittitle: Patti Smith Concert in Bologna
Celebration Before Elections

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting a spring festival (Festa di Primavera) in Piazza Maggiore organized by the "Lista del Sole" (Italian Green party) before regional elections.

did.unittitle: Spring festival during regional elections / Festa di primavera in Piazza Maggiore, Bologna
Gay Pride in Bologna

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of the National Gay Pride Rally, Bologna, Italy, June 28, 1980.

did.unittitle: National Gay Pride Rally / Raduno Nazionale dell' Orgoglio Omosessuale, Bologna
Squats in Bologna

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs documenting the occupation of buildings by the Unione Inquilini (Tenants Union) of Bologna.

did.unittitle: Occupation of apartments by the Tenants Union / Occupazione casa da parte dell' Unione, Bologna
